findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete new assertions that must be added to `test_metrics_docs.py`: at minimum (a) `docs/config.md` contains a `[code_health]` section with `size_cap` and `size_near_fraction` keys, (b) `docs/reuse-surface.md` mentions `scc` and `jscpd` as PATH prerequisites distinct from the `[metrics]` pip extra, (c) `docs/reuse-surface.md` names `lizard` as the sole pip dep in the extra, and (d) the analyzer seam (e.g. `AnalyzerResult`) is referenced in `docs/reuse-surface.md`.
evidence: Existing test_metrics_docs.py (read in full): four assertions — janitor discovery, user-guide Metrics section (4 lenses + 'unavailable'), reuse-surface references 'rebar.metrics', AGENTS.md pointer. None cover docs/config.md content.
AC1 requires: 'docs/reuse-surface.md: analyzer seam + the [metrics] extra (lizard only) + scc/jscpd as separate PATH prerequisites' — no test assertion planned for these.
AC2 requires: 'docs/config.md: [code_health] keys/types/defaults' — no test assertion planned to verify config.md was updated.
Plan says 'EXTEND it' but gives zero specification of what assertions to add — an executor writing only a comment '# extended' would satisfy the literal AC.
findings.finding: The test extension spec in AC4 does not enumerate what new assertions must be added to test_metrics_docs.py, leaving the verification step structurally unable to cover the new content deliverables in AC1+AC2 (docs/config.md [code_health] section, scc/jscpd as PATH prereqs in reuse-surface.md, lizard-only [metrics] extra, analyzer seam). The existing test file (confirmed by read) checks only the four 121b assertions; none of them touch config.md or the metrics extra/PATH prereqs. A passing test run after this ticket would NOT prove AC1+AC2 content is correct.
findings.location: AC4: 'tests/scripts/test_metrics_docs.py extended; existing assertions still pass' and Verification: 'test_metrics_docs.py passes (old + new assertions)'
scenarios: Implementer adds docs/config.md [code_health] section with wrong key names or missing defaults; test suite passes anyway.
Implementer omits the scc/jscpd PATH prerequisite distinction from docs/reuse-surface.md; test suite passes anyway.
Implementer documents lizard as a PATH binary rather than pip dep in reuse-surface.md; test suite passes anyway.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC4: 'New assertions must cover: (a) docs/config.md contains `[code_health]` and at least the keys `size_cap`/`size_near_fraction`; (b) docs/reuse-surface.md contains `scc` and `jscpd` as PATH prerequisites; (c) docs/reuse-surface.md names `lizard` as the only pip dep in the `[metrics]` extra; (d) docs/reuse-surface.md references the analyzer seam (AnalyzerResult or guard_import).'

evidence: vague/subjective terms present: clean
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact docs lint command in the Verification section (e.g. `markdownlint docs/` or `make lint-docs`) so the step is unambiguously executable.
evidence: Plan text: 'test_metrics_docs.py passes (old + new assertions); docs lint clean'
ABSENCE: no lint tool (e.g. markdownlint, vale, mdformat --check) or invocation is specified
No reference to a project-level lint config or Makefile target
findings.finding: The Verification step 'docs lint clean' names no concrete lint command or tool, leaving the executor to choose an arbitrary linter that may not match project conventions.
scenarios: An agent runs a linter that is not the project standard and passes, while the actual CI linter would fail.
An agent skips docs lint because it cannot determine which command to run.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'docs lint clean' with the project's canonical invocation, e.g. `markdownlint docs/reuse-surface.md docs/config.md docs/user-guide.md` or `make lint-docs`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the conditional AGENTS.md AC with a concrete falsifiable check: state the current one-liner value (or reference the 0734 output), specify the expected new value if changed, and add a proving command (e.g. `grep -n 'metrics' AGENTS.md | diff - expected_snippet`) so both the 'update needed' and 'no update needed' branches produce verifiable evidence.
evidence: Plan text: 'AGENTS.md metrics pointer updated if the one-liner changed'
Verification section lists only test_metrics_docs.py and docs lint — no AGENTS.md check is mentioned
ABSENCE: no proving command (e.g. grep or diff) is given for the AGENTS.md criterion in either branch of the conditional
findings.finding: The AGENTS.md AC is conditional ('updated if the one-liner changed'), deferring the decision of whether an update is needed to the executor with no proving command for either branch. An agent can mark this done by declaring no change occurred, with nothing falsifiable.
findings.location: Acceptance criteria — 'AGENTS.md metrics pointer updated if the one-liner changed'
scenarios: Agent checks AGENTS.md, decides 'the one-liner didn't change', skips the update, and closes the ticket — no evidence either way is recorded.
One-liner DID change (due to ticket 0734) but agent misses it because there is no concrete comparison step.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the AC to: 'AGENTS.md: confirm the metrics one-liner matches the post-0734 canonical form; if it differs, update it and record the diff in the PR.' Add to Verification: `grep -A1 'metrics' AGENTS.md` showing expected output.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a verification mechanism for AC3: either (a) add an assertion in test_metrics_docs.py that checks AGENTS.md contains the expected metrics pointer text, or (b) make the criterion unconditional (always verify the pointer matches the current one-liner) to eliminate the unverifiable conditional.
evidence: AC3: 'AGENTS.md metrics pointer updated if the one-liner changed'
Verification: 'test_metrics_docs.py passes (old + new assertions); docs lint clean' — no assertion covers AGENTS.md content
findings.finding: AC3 ('AGENTS.md metrics pointer updated if the one-liner changed') is a conditional criterion with no corresponding verification mechanism — neither the Verification step nor the test file extension is specified to check it.
scenarios: An implementer skips the AGENTS.md update because the condition is unclear and there is no test to catch the omission.
findings.suggested_fix: In test_metrics_docs.py, add a check that reads AGENTS.md and asserts the metrics one-liner/pointer matches the canonical text defined in the docs. This removes the 'if' ambiguity and gives the criterion a concrete verification path.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define what 'analyzer seam' means in AC1: specify which interface (e.g. `AnalyzerResult` from ticket 3b30/0734, the `guard_import(extra="metrics")` call site, the adapter protocol in `analyzers/`) and which attributes/methods must be documented in `docs/reuse-surface.md`.
evidence: Plan phrase 'analyzer seam' appears once (AC1) with no definition or pointer to the interface shape.
Ticket 0734 defines AnalyzerResult.loc (per-file LOC) as the relevant output, but the plan does not reference this; ticket 3b30 (the interface creator) is not mentioned in this plan at all.
docs/reuse-surface.md currently contains no mention of 'metrics', 'analyzer', 'AnalyzerResult', 'MetricSpec', or 'REGISTRY' (confirmed by search) — the executor has no baseline to extend from.
findings.finding: The 'analyzer seam' mentioned in AC1 ('docs/reuse-surface.md: analyzer seam') is never defined in the plan — what interface/contract constitutes the 'seam' to document is left entirely implicit, with no pointer to which attributes of AnalyzerResult (from ticket 0734/3b30) must appear in the documentation. An implementer reading only this plan cannot determine what to write.
findings.location: AC1: 'docs/reuse-surface.md: analyzer seam + the [metrics] extra (lizard only) + scc/jscpd as separate PATH prerequisites'
scenarios: Implementer documents only the high-level import path (`rebar.metrics`) without the AnalyzerResult shape, leaving library consumers unable to use the seam.
Implementer documents 0734's LOC-only view but omits complexity/duplication fields that ticket 9597's lizard integration adds.

findings.finding: The plan's AC1 and Description say to document the `[metrics]` extra in docs/reuse-surface.md and docs/user-guide.md, but `[metrics]` does not appear as a key in pyproject.toml `[project.optional-dependencies]` or in `src/rebar/_optional.py`'s `EXTRAS` dict. The creating ticket 0734 is recorded only as `relates_to` (not `depends_on`), so the formal resolution order does not guarantee 0734 lands before this docs ticket, leaving the executor documenting an extra that may not yet exist.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the dependency edge from `relates_to` to `depends_on` for ticket 0734, or add 0734 to the formal 'Dependencies' line alongside the note that it is the content prerequisite.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit note in the plan (or a test guard) that the `[metrics]` extra documentation content must be validated against 9597's merged `pyproject.toml` state before merging this doc ticket — e.g., require `'metrics' in pyproject.toml [project.optional-dependencies]` as a precondition check in `test_metrics_docs.py`.
evidence: Plan states: 'A [metrics] pip EXTRA IS created by ticket 9597 and contains ONLY lizard'
src/rebar/_optional.py EXTRAS dict contains: 'agents', 'eval', 'tracing', 'grounding' — no 'metrics' key
pyproject.toml optional-dependencies section has no metrics entry (verified via search)
Ticket 9597 status: OPEN (not yet merged)
Evidence class: PARTIALLY-VERIFIED — the integration surface exists in 9597's plan but not yet in the codebase
findings.finding: The plan asserts `pip install rebar[metrics]` as a documented adoption path, but the `[metrics]` extra does not yet exist in `src/rebar/_optional.py` EXTRAS or `pyproject.toml` — it is being created by the still-open blocking ticket 9597. The plan presents this as a verified grounded fact ('A [metrics] pip EXTRA IS created by ticket 9597') but 9597 is unmerged; if its final shape changes the extra name, probe module, or pip package name, the documentation this plan produces will mischaracterize the actual install surface.
scenarios: If 9597 ships with a different extra name (e.g., 'code-health' instead of 'metrics'), docs produced by this ticket will direct users to a non-existent extra.
If 9597 is revised to bundle additional packages alongside lizard, the 'ONLY lizard' characterization in docs/reuse-surface.md will be inaccurate.
test_metrics_docs.py new assertions asserting the install path will pass even if the actual extra never lands (doc tests check text strings, not installability).
findings.suggested_fix: Add a test assertion in `tests/scripts/test_metrics_docs.py` that reads `pyproject.toml` and confirms `metrics` is present in `[project.optional-dependencies]`, so the test suite fails (rather than silently passing) if this doc ticket is merged before 9597.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit assertion requirement to the test extension: the new test assertions must include at least one check that user-guide.md contains the 'no analyzer → Unavailable' fallback wording (e.g. `assert 'Unavailable' in user_guide_text`), and name this expectation in the AC or Verification section.
evidence: AC2 documents the fallback behavior in user-guide.md
AC4 says 'extended' without naming which new assertions are required
Verification only states 'old + new assertions' without linking new assertions to the fallback claim
ABSENCE: no AC or verification step explicitly requires a grep/assertion proving the fallback phrase appears in user-guide.md
findings.finding: The AC requires test_metrics_docs.py to be 'extended' but does not specify that the new assertions must cover the 'no analyzer → Unavailable' fallback behavior documented in user-guide.md, leaving that documented claim unverified by any test.
findings.location: Acceptance criteria — 'tests/scripts/test_metrics_docs.py extended' and 'docs/user-guide.md Metrics section: … "no analyzer -> Unavailable" behavior'
scenarios: Executor extends the test with assertions about install instructions only, leaving the 'Unavailable' fallback language unchecked — the AC is marked done but the fallback documentation is never verified.
findings.suggested_fix: In AC4, change to: 'tests/scripts/test_metrics_docs.py extended with assertions covering: (a) three-part install instructions in user-guide.md, (b) [code_health] keys in config.md, (c) the "no analyzer → Unavailable" fallback phrase in user-guide.md; existing assertions still pass.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Make the AGENTS.md update unconditional: replace 'if the one-liner changed' with a mandatory verification step (e.g., 'verify AGENTS.md metrics pointer reflects the current three-part install') so it cannot be silently skipped.
evidence: AC line: 'AGENTS.md metrics pointer updated if the one-liner changed.'
The description documents a new three-part install model (extra + scc + jscpd) that almost certainly changes any prior one-liner; an ambiguous conditional creates a loophole to skip the update.
T7(b): 'The change doesn't strand stale references' — a skipped AGENTS.md update would strand the old one-liner.
findings.finding: The AGENTS.md update is conditioned on 'if the one-liner changed,' which may cause the update to be skipped even when the metrics one-liner DID change, leaving AGENTS.md with a stale reference.
findings.location: Acceptance criteria: 'AGENTS.md metrics pointer updated if the one-liner changed.'
scenarios: A developer reads AGENTS.md after this ticket lands, finds the old one-liner (e.g., 'pip install rebar'), and follows it — missing the scc/jscpd PATH prerequisites entirely.
Reviewer approves the ticket without touching AGENTS.md because they judge the one-liner 'didn't change enough,' leaving the pointer stale.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the AC to: 'AGENTS.md metrics pointer verified/updated to reflect the three-part install (pip install rebar[metrics] + scc + jscpd on PATH).' This removes the conditional and turns it into a required audit step regardless of what changed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either remove the `docs/config.md` deliverable from this leaf's AC2 and scope (align to parent 121b's file set), or update parent 121b to explicitly include `docs/config.md` as a file and AC (which stales 121b's plan-review attestation and requires its re-review before this leaf is approved).
evidence: Parent 121b Files section lists only: examples/agent-skills/rebar-janitor/phases/discovery.md, docs/user-guide.md, docs/reuse-surface.md, AGENTS.md — docs/config.md is absent.
Parent 121b ACs make no mention of [code_health] config keys, types, or defaults.

Parent 121b Tests section asserts only: rebar metrics reference in discovery.md, Metrics section in user-guide.md with four lens names + unavailable state, AGENTS.md pointer, reuse-surface rebar.metrics reference.
Leaf plan Description and AC2 explicitly add: 'docs/config.md: [code_health] keys/types/defaults' — new surface not in the parent contract.
findings.finding: The leaf delivers docs/config.md ([code_health] keys/types/defaults) — a file and deliverable type entirely absent from parent 121b's declared scope, file list, ACs, and Tests.
scenarios: An implementer delivering docs/config.md edits would be stepping outside the parent story's defined scope, risking merge conflicts or double-documentation if the parent story or a sibling delivers config docs separately.
The parent epic (86ac) is already closed; adding docs/config.md content under a child of 121b (which is also closed) without a parent-level contract creates an orphaned deliverable.
findings.suggested_fix: If docs/config.md documentation of [code_health] config keys is genuinely needed, add it to parent 121b's Files list and ACs (updating the parent), then re-review 121b before proceeding with this leaf. If the parent is already closed, raise a new sibling ticket under the correct parent rather than expanding this leaf beyond 121b's scope.

findings.finding: AC1 specifies documenting 'the optional `[metrics]` extra + external CLIs (scc, jscpd via npx)' but no `[metrics]` extra exists in `pyproject.toml` or `_optional.py`'s `EXTRAS` dict, and ticket 0734 (the declared content prerequisite) does not appear to create one — its files-touched are `git_metrics.py`, `registry.py`, `analyzer.py`, `test_metrics_cli.py` with no `pyproject.toml` change. If the final shape from 0734 does not include a `[metrics]` pip extra (and scc/jscpd are instead PATH-binary prerequisites with no extra), documenting it as a pip extra would mislead adopters into running `pip install nava-rebar[metrics]` against a non-existent extra.
findings.location: AC line 1: 'docs/reuse-surface.md metrics section: analyzer seam + `[metrics]` extra + external CLIs'

findings.finding: AC1 names 'the optional `[metrics]` extra' as a documentable surface, but `src/rebar/_optional.py`'s EXTRAS dict contains only 'agents', 'eval', 'tracing', and 'grounding' — no 'metrics' entry — and `pyproject.toml` has no `[metrics]` optional-dependency group. scc and jscpd are external PATH binaries (not pip deps, per the pyproject.toml comment 'out-of-process backends … are external CLIs, not pip deps'). Documenting a `[metrics]` extra as if it exists would publish a false capability claim to adopters.
findings.location: AC1: 'docs/reuse-surface.md metrics section: analyzer seam + `[metrics]` extra + external CLIs'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'optional `[metrics]` extra' in AC1 with the accurate framing: scc is an external PATH binary (fail-open when absent, same pattern as the grounding engine) and jscpd is invoked via `npx` (Node.js toolchain prerequisite). If the dep chain does introduce a `[metrics]` extra (i.e. if 21fb/3ba0 add `lizard` as a pip dep behind `guard_import(..., extra='metrics')`), the plan must first confirm that entry exists in `_optional.EXTRAS` before documenting it.

findings.finding: The plan documents a `[metrics]` optional extra (AC: 'docs/reuse-surface.md metrics section: analyzer seam + `[metrics]` extra + external CLIs'), but no such extra exists in pyproject.toml, and the project's established pattern explicitly treats scc and other external CLIs as SYSTEM prerequisites — not pip extras. The grounding extra comment states 'The out-of-process backends (universal-ctags, OpenGrep/ast-grep, scc/lizard) are external CLIs, not pip deps.' Documenting a pip extra that gates non-pip system tools would cargo-cult the extras mechanism inconsistently.
findings.location: AC line 1: 'docs/reuse-surface.md metrics section: analyzer seam + `[metrics]` extra + external CLIs'
findings.suggested_fix: If no pip packages are being gated, drop the `[metrics]` extra framing and document scc + jscpd/npx as system prerequisites (the same way `acli` is documented for Jira reconciliation), consistent with the grounding extra's treatment of 'external CLIs, not pip deps'.

findings.finding: The plan's Description and AC1 explicitly call for documenting the '[metrics]' pip extra, but no such extra exists in pyproject.toml or in _optional.py's EXTRAS registry. Ticket 0734 (the named content prerequisite) also does not create this extra — it focuses on wiring metric specs to the analyzer. Documenting 'pip install nava-rebar[metrics]' or referencing guard_import(..., extra='metrics') would describe a non-existent installation surface.
findings.location: Description: 'the optional [metrics] extra'; AC1: 'docs/reuse-surface.md metrics section: analyzer seam + [metrics] extra + external CLIs'
findings.suggested_fix: If scc and jscpd are external PATH binaries (not pip packages), the docs section should describe them as CLI prerequisites (install via your OS package manager / npm), NOT as a pip extra. Remove '[metrics] extra' from AC1 unless a concrete dependency ticket creates that extra in pyproject.toml + _optional.py.

findings.finding: The plan says to document 'jscpd via npx' as the invocation mode, but the current codebase's only jscpd caller (scripts/backfill_complexity_clone_snapshots.py:72) invokes bare ['jscpd', ...], not ['npx', 'jscpd', ...]. The npx-based invocation is the target mandated by adapter ticket 3ba0, which is a dep of 0734. If this docs ticket is implemented before 3ba0/0734 fully land, the documented CLI invocation pattern will not match the actual code path.
findings.location: Description: 'external-CLI prerequisites (scc, jscpd via npx)'; AC1: 'docs/reuse-surface.md metrics section: analyzer seam + [metrics] extra + external CLIs'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit AC bullet requiring that docs/user-guide.md's code_health lens description be updated to remove `cap_change_events` (deregistered by 0734) and that `test_metrics_docs.py` asserts it is absent from the Metrics section.
evidence: docs/user-guide.md line 252: 'cap-change events' is listed as part of the code_health lens

Ticket 0734 Description: 'DECISION: REMOVE it from the portable code_health registry (deregister the spec). It may survive as a rebar-only opt-in... but it is NOT part of the client-facing analyzer stack. State the removal explicitly in the changelog note.'
Plan AC says 'docs/user-guide.md Metrics section: the three-part install...' but does not mention reconciling cap_change_events removal
findings.finding: The plan's AC for docs/user-guide.md does not specify removing the stale `cap_change_events` reference that appears in the already-landed Metrics section (user-guide.md line 252: 'cap-change events'). Ticket 0734 (the content prerequisite) explicitly deregisters cap_change_events from the portable code_health registry. If the implementer extends the Metrics section without removing this reference, the documentation will assert a metric that no longer exists in the registry.
findings.location: Acceptance criteria: docs/user-guide.md Metrics section; docs/user-guide.md line 252
scenarios: Implementer extends the Metrics section to add the three-part install and scc/jscpd notes without updating the code_health lens enumeration — shipped docs name a removed metric.
test_metrics_docs.py extended to assert lens names but doesn't assert cap_change_events is absent — the stale reference passes tests undetected.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the docs/user-guide.md AC: 'cap_change_events is NOT listed in the code_health lens description (deregistered by 0734).' Add a corresponding negative assertion in test_metrics_docs.py: `assert 'cap_change_events' not in text` (or assert only the surviving lens members are named).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the user-guide.md Metrics section spec to require documentation of each partial-installation state (lizard-only, scc-only, jscpd-only, and pairwise combinations), specifying whether each yields a degraded/partial result or a full Unavailable.
evidence: AC specifies only 'no analyzer -> Unavailable' as the documented error state
Three separate prerequisites (lizard via pip, scc on PATH, jscpd on PATH) are each independently installable and independently absent-able
Plan does not require docs to specify what happens when lizard is present but scc is missing, or when scc is present but jscpd is missing
findings.finding: The UX documentation spec covers the all-absent case ('no analyzer -> Unavailable') but does not require the docs to address partial-installation states — e.g., lizard installed but scc or jscpd missing from PATH, or vice versa — leaving users without guidance on what behavior to expect when only some of the three prerequisites are present.
findings.location: docs/user-guide.md Metrics section — AC line: 'the three-part install (extra + scc + jscpd) + "no analyzer -> Unavailable" behavior'
scenarios: User installs rebar[metrics] but forgets to add scc to PATH — unclear whether they get a partial metrics report or a full Unavailable
User has scc but not jscpd — no documented behavior means silent partial failure or confusing error message is undiscoverable from docs
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the AC for docs/user-guide.md: 'Document per-tool absence behavior: if any individual prerequisite (lizard, scc, jscpd) is missing, state whether the affected metric is individually marked Unavailable while others proceed, or whether the entire metrics feature is suppressed. Extend test_metrics_docs.py to assert these per-tool absence scenarios are covered.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
